scoringModel.multipleSelect: partial credit = correct selections ratio minus wrong selections penalty, floored at 0
scoringModel.weightedAnalytics: category and skill analytics use item scoringWeight metadata
scoringModel.confidenceAnalytics: candidate confidence is recorded before submit and compared with correctness
scoringModel.sessionProtocol: runtime sessions store per-item timing, answer history and confidence-adjusted score contributions
scoringModel.retirementRules: items are flagged for retirement if facility, discrimination or expert review evidence falls outside production thresholds
scoringModel.runtimeBankLoading: the standalone app can import a compatible JSON item bank without editing HTML
scoringModel.pilotPsychometrics: pilot response CSV/JSON files can be converted into facility, discrimination and retirement-review signals
scoringModel.expertReviewWorkflow: manual review packet, review queue, reviewer CSV template, adjudication validator and anti-pattern reports gate future bank edits
scoringModel.secureDelivery: server-backed prototype redacts candidate payloads, requires per-attempt candidate tokens, stores token hashes only, persists attempts locally, records audit events, scores responses server-side and protects evaluator bank/report access with a runtime authorization boundary

sourcePolicy: Source horizons identify article-level 2022-2026 research contexts; assessment passages are original and not copied from journal text.
limitations: psychometric values are targets until pilot response data is collected
offline HTML cannot provide secure server-side answer-key separation; use the server-backed delivery prototype for redacted candidate payloads
expert review metadata indicates review gates, not completed independent human adjudication
PWA installation requires serving the generated files over http(s), not opening the HTML through file://
psychometric calibration requires real pilot candidate responses; empty reports are readiness placeholders, not validation evidence

manualReviewChecklist: rhetorical function is not reducible to topic recognition
items.retirementRule: Retire or rewrite if pilot facility is above .88, below .20, discrimination is below .20, or expert review marks the key as ambiguous.
items.sourceHorizon: Nature, 2024 - Accurate structure prediction of biomolecular interactions with AlphaFold 3 (https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-024-07487-w)
items.passage: All-atom prediction of biomolecular complexes is treated as a test of intellectual discipline rather than as a simple announcement of technical progress. It first acknowledges that a diffusion-based architecture for joint modelling of proteins, nucleic acids, ligands and ions has changed what can be attempted in computational structural biology, particularly for drug-discovery triage. Yet the argument then slows down: benchmarking across molecular interaction classes is presented as consequential evidence, not as a warrant for unrestricted inference. The crucial turn is the author's insistence that confidence scores do not remove the need for physical validation of rare or modified systems. That turn prevents the reader from confusing interface confidence with final explanation. By placing training-set proximity near the end, the passage converts excitement into a conditional claim about where the method travels, where it does not, and what kind of validation remains necessary. A careful reader must therefore distinguish evidential usefulness from unrestricted portability, especially when training-set proximity may alter the observed pattern.
items.prompt: Which option best captures the passage's central rhetorical function?
options: It claims that modern computational structural biology now permits every result to be generalized immediately to all populations and policy settings.

It says that training-set proximity is caused by a diffusion-based architecture for joint modelling of proteins, nucleic acids, ligands and ions, so the method should be rejected before interface confidence is measured.
It asserts that benchmarking across molecular interaction classes has conclusively and permanently verified all-atom prediction of biomolecular complexes beyond all methodological dispute.
It acknowledges the methodological advance while making the evidential claim conditional on scope, validation and training-set proximity.
It mainly proposes a funding and governance framework for drug-discovery triage, although the passage is actually about evidential scope.
items.rationale: The correct answer is D because it preserves the passage's concessive structure: a diffusion-based architecture for joint modelling of proteins, nucleic acids, ligands and ions is valuable, but the warrant is limited by scope, validation and training-set proximity. The false-synchrony distractor reverses the relation between method and limitation. The overgeneralisation distractor treats a bounded case in computational structural biology as a universal rule. The modal-distortion distractor turns qualified evidence into permanent verification. The out-of-context distractor is plausible policy prose, but it does not answer the rhetorical question.

items.passage: Probabilistic medium-range weather prediction is important in the passage, but the author resists the rhetorical shortcut by which methodological novelty becomes institutional certainty. The account of a generative model trained on decades of atmospheric reanalysis is deliberately balanced against comparison with operational ensemble forecasts, and the balance matters because rare compound events remain harder to validate than average skill scores imply. The most revealing sentence is not the one praising the approach, but the one that narrows its scope to global ten-day forecast cases. The passage therefore asks the reader to see ensemble reliability as a disciplined indicator. It is useful for ranking, probing or comparing claims, but it cannot neutralize archive-induced stationarity or settle every question relevant to early-warning systems. The passage rewards attention to modality: it treats the method as consequential, but not as a substitute for validation in global ten-day forecast cases. This is why the strongest answer must preserve scope, causal direction and the difference between a research signal and a settled explanation.
items.prompt: What is the most defensible inference about the author's stance?
options: The author values the method, but treats ensemble reliability as a situated signal rather than as proof that early-warning systems can proceed without further scrutiny.
It says that archive-induced stationarity is caused by a generative model trained on decades of atmospheric reanalysis, so the method should be rejected before ensemble reliability is measured.
It mainly proposes a funding and governance framework for early-warning systems, although the passage is actually about evidential scope.
It claims that modern climate informatics now permits every result to be generalized immediately to all populations and policy settings.
It asserts that comparison with operational ensemble forecasts has conclusively and permanently verified probabilistic medium-range weather prediction beyond all methodological dispute.
items.rationale: The correct answer is A because it preserves the passage's concessive structure: a generative model trained on decades of atmospheric reanalysis is valuable, but the warrant is limited by scope, validation and archive-induced stationarity. The false-synchrony distractor reverses the relation between method and limitation. The overgeneralisation distractor treats a bounded case in climate informatics as a universal rule. The modal-distortion distractor turns qualified evidence into permanent verification. The out-of-context distractor is plausible policy prose, but it does not answer the rhetorical question.

items.sourceHorizon: Nature, 2025 - End-to-end data-driven weather prediction (https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-025-08897-0)
items.passage: Weather prediction without conventional numerical-weather inputs is framed through concession rather than celebration. On one side, atmospheric modelling has gained a method - an end-to-end system mapping raw observations directly to forecasts - that makes the problem more tractable than it was in earlier research cycles. On the other side, the passage refuses to treat tractability as proof. It uses forecast comparisons against conventional numerical weather prediction to establish that the approach has empirical substance, then uses operational independence does not by itself prove robustness under sensor failures to limit the inference. This structure matters because the likely misreading is seductive: if forecast lead-time skill improves, one might assume that rapid operational forecasting can proceed without further scrutiny. The passage rejects precisely that inference, especially where observation-network bias remains plausible. This is why the strongest answer must preserve scope, causal direction and the difference between a research signal and a settled explanation.
items.prompt: Why does the passage foreground the methodological caveat?
options: It claims that modern atmospheric modelling now permits every result to be generalized immediately to all populations and policy settings.

It asserts that forecast comparisons against conventional numerical weather prediction has conclusively and permanently verified weather prediction without conventional numerical-weather inputs beyond all methodological dispute.
It says that observation-network bias is caused by an end-to-end system mapping raw observations directly to forecasts, so the method should be rejected before forecast lead-time skill is measured.
It mainly proposes a funding and governance framework for rapid operational forecasting, although the passage is actually about evidential scope.
The caveat prevents forecast comparisons against conventional numerical weather prediction from being read as unrestricted proof across global weather forecasts and adjacent settings.
items.rationale: The correct answer is E because it preserves the passage's concessive structure: an end-to-end system mapping raw observations directly to forecasts is valuable, but the warrant is limited by scope, validation and observation-network bias. The false-synchrony distractor reverses the relation between method and limitation. The overgeneralisation distractor treats a bounded case in atmospheric modelling as a universal rule. The modal-distortion distractor turns qualified evidence into permanent verification. The out-of-context distractor is plausible policy prose, but it does not answer the rhetorical question.

items.passage: General forecasting across atmospheric and environmental tasks gives the author's argument a diagnostic rather than celebratory shape. A method associated with the topic has widened the possible questions in earth-system AI, but it has also exposed how easily readers confuse evidence with authority. The discussion of multi-task comparisons across forecasting benchmarks is therefore framed by a caution about heterogeneous Earth-system datasets. The author is not saying that pretraining on diverse Earth-system data before task-specific adaptation is weak; rather, the author is saying that cross-task transfer skill has to be interpreted against breadth of task coverage can conceal uneven performance in local extremes. The resulting stance is neither sceptical dismissal nor promotional certainty. It is a calibrated position: the method can guide foundation-model forecasting, provided that benchmark selection is treated as an evidential problem, not an inconvenience. The relevant academic skill is not recognising the topic, but tracking how the warrant changes when breadth of task coverage can conceal uneven performance in local extremes.
items.prompt: Which statement best synthesizes the passage's argument without overstating it?
options: It says that benchmark selection is caused by pretraining on diverse Earth-system data before task-specific adaptation, so the method should be rejected before cross-task transfer skill is measured.
It mainly proposes a funding and governance framework for foundation-model forecasting, although the passage is actually about evidential scope.

It asserts that multi-task comparisons across forecasting benchmarks has conclusively and permanently verified general forecasting across atmospheric and environmental tasks beyond all methodological dispute.
The passage argues that general forecasting across atmospheric and environmental tasks is promising precisely because it is useful under specified conditions, not because it removes uncertainty.
It claims that modern earth-system AI now permits every result to be generalized immediately to all populations and policy settings.
items.rationale: The correct answer is D because it preserves the passage's concessive structure: pretraining on diverse Earth-system data before task-specific adaptation is valuable, but the warrant is limited by scope, validation and benchmark selection. The false-synchrony distractor reverses the relation between method and limitation. The overgeneralisation distractor treats a bounded case in earth-system AI as a universal rule. items.passage: Weather prediction without conventional numerical-weather inputs is treated as a test of intellectual discipline rather than as a simple announcement of technical progress. It first acknowledges that an end-to-end system mapping raw observations directly to forecasts has changed what can be attempted in atmospheric modelling, particularly for rapid operational forecasting. The crucial turn is the author's insistence that operational independence does not by itself prove robustness under sensor failures. That turn prevents the reader from confusing forecast lead-time skill with final explanation. By placing observation-network bias near the end, the passage converts excitement into a conditional claim about where the method travels, where it does not, and what kind of validation remains necessary. A careful reader must therefore distinguish evidential usefulness from unrestricted portability, especially when observation-network bias may alter the observed pattern.

The relevant academic skill is not recognising the topic, but tracking how the warrant changes when market prediction improves triage without replacing independent replication. A careful reader must therefore distinguish evidential usefulness from unrestricted portability, especially when original-study selection may alter the observed pattern.
items.prompt: What role does the counterweight in the final third of the passage play?

items.rationale: The correct answer is A because it preserves the passage's concessive structure: preregistered replications selected with prediction-market information is valuable, but the warrant is limited by scope, validation and original-study selection. The false-synchrony distractor reverses the relation between method and limitation. The overgeneralisation distractor treats a bounded case in meta-science as a universal rule. The modal-distortion distractor turns qualified evidence into permanent verification. The out-of-context distractor is plausible policy prose, but it does not answer the rhetorical question.

items.sourceHorizon: Nature, 2024 - Accurate structure prediction of biomolecular interactions with AlphaFold 3 (https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-024-07487-w)
items.passage: A draft abstract about all-atom prediction of biomolecular complexes states that a diffusion-based architecture for joint modelling of proteins, nucleic acids, ligands and ions "proves" the central finding and that drug-discovery triage should follow immediately. The draft is forceful, but its force is partly grammatical rather than evidential. The described study actually rests on benchmarking across molecular interaction classes, and its own limitations section states that confidence scores do not remove the need for physical validation of rare or modified systems. A stronger academic version would keep the result visible while lowering the certainty of the verbs. It would also preserve the concessive relation between interface confidence and training-set proximity. The central editing task is to avoid two opposite failures: a timid rewrite that erases the finding, and an inflated rewrite that treats protein-ligand and protein-nucleic-acid systems as if they represented every relevant setting. A careful reader must therefore distinguish evidential usefulness from unrestricted portability, especially when training-set proximity may alter the observed pattern.
items.prompt: Which revisions preserve the abstract's academic stance and syntactic logic? Select TWO.
options: In the opening claim, replace absolute verbs such as "proves" with calibrated verbs such as "suggests", "supports", or "is consistent with".
Replace the cautious verb in the opening claim with "guarantees", making interface confidence sound like a final institutional mandate.

Rewrite training-set proximity as an effect of the method, making the sentence read as though the caveat were produced by a diffusion-based architecture for joint modelling of proteins, nucleic acids, ligands and ions.
Retain the limiting clause about training-set proximity, because it affects interpretation rather than merely style.
Delete the limitation that confidence scores do not remove the need for physical validation of rare or modified systems, because a limitation weakens the abstract's persuasive force.
items.rationale: The correct answer set is A, D. These choices preserve the claim while reducing over-certainty in the opening claim, keeping training-set proximity as an interpretive constraint and, when required, limiting transfer beyond protein-ligand and protein-nucleic-acid systems. The incorrect options enact three traps: deleting the caveat is overgeneralisation, changing "may inform" to "must determine" is modal inflation, and making the caveat an effect of the method creates false synchrony. items.passage: Probabilistic medium-range weather prediction is described in a paragraph that is syntactically polished but epistemically overheated. It locates the work in climate informatics, reports comparison with operational ensemble forecasts, and then jumps from this evidence to a broad recommendation about early-warning systems. The problem is not the recommendation itself; it is the unlicensed bridge from measured ensemble reliability to general validity. Because rare compound events remain harder to validate than average skill scores imply, a precise revision must use verbs such as "suggests", "is consistent with", or "supports further evaluation" rather than verbs such as "settles" or "guarantees". It must also keep archive-induced stationarity in the sentence as a possible threat to interpretation rather than reclassifying it as an outcome. The passage rewards attention to modality: it treats the method as consequential, but not as a substitute for validation in global ten-day forecast cases. This is why the strongest answer must preserve scope, causal direction and the difference between a research signal and a settled explanation.

Change the opening claim from "may inform early-warning systems" to "must determine early-warning systems", as though the evidence had removed the need for further validation.
Rewrite archive-induced stationarity as an effect of the method, making the sentence read as though the caveat were produced by a generative model trained on decades of atmospheric reanalysis.
items.rationale: The correct answer set is A, B, C. They should also keep the syntax of concession intact: the observed result matters, although observation-network bias still limits how far the conclusion can travel beyond global weather forecasts. This is why the strongest answer must preserve scope, causal direction and the difference between a research signal and a settled explanation. items.passage: A draft abstract about probabilistic medium-range weather prediction states that a generative model trained on decades of atmospheric reanalysis "proves" the central finding and that early-warning systems should follow immediately. The draft is forceful, but its force is partly grammatical rather than evidential. The described study actually rests on comparison with operational ensemble forecasts, and its own limitations section states that rare compound events remain harder to validate than average skill scores imply. A stronger academic version would keep the result visible while lowering the certainty of the verbs. It would also preserve the concessive relation between ensemble reliability and archive-induced stationarity. The central editing task is to avoid two opposite failures: a timid rewrite that erases the finding, and an inflated rewrite that treats global ten-day forecast cases as if they represented every relevant setting. The relevant academic skill is not recognising the topic, but tracking how the warrant changes when rare compound events remain harder to validate than average skill scores imply.
items.prompt: Which expressions would improve precision without changing the author's claim? Select THREE.
options: In the scope sentence, replace absolute verbs such as "proves" with calibrated verbs such as "suggests", "supports", or "is consistent with".
Change the scope sentence from "may inform early-warning systems" to "must determine early-warning systems", as though the evidence had removed the need for further validation.
Specify in the scope sentence that the claim applies first to global ten-day forecast cases, with transfer beyond that context requiring further validation.
Retain the limiting clause about archive-induced stationarity, because it affects interpretation rather than merely style.
Rewrite archive-induced stationarity as an effect of the method, making the sentence read as though the caveat were produced by a generative model trained on decades of atmospheric reanalysis.
items.rationale: The correct answer set is A, C, D. These choices preserve the claim while reducing over-certainty in the scope sentence, keeping archive-induced stationarity as an interpretive constraint and, when required, limiting transfer beyond global ten-day forecast cases. The incorrect options enact two traps: changing "may inform" to "must determine" is modal inflation, and making the caveat an effect of the method creates false synchrony. items.passage: A draft abstract about weather prediction without conventional numerical-weather inputs states that an end-to-end system mapping raw observations directly to forecasts "proves" the central finding and that rapid operational forecasting should follow immediately. A stronger academic version would keep the result visible while lowering the certainty of the verbs. It would also preserve the concessive relation between forecast lead-time skill and observation-network bias. The central editing task is to avoid two opposite failures: a timid rewrite that erases the finding, and an inflated rewrite that treats global weather forecasts as if they represented every relevant setting. This is why the strongest answer must preserve scope, causal direction and the difference between a research signal and a settled explanation.

dataVisualization.reviewerCheck: Candidate must integrate direction, uncertainty, sensitivity and missingness rather than reading the estimate alone.

items.passage: A study in exoplanet spectroscopy examines methane and vertical mixing in a warm Neptune atmosphere in the context of warm Neptune spectra. It uses JWST transmission spectroscopy with atmospheric retrieval and reports molecular detections and abundance estimates for WASP-107b. The primary analysis includes 240 observations and estimates an adjusted change of 0.07 units, with a 95% confidence interval from 0.03 to 0.15. Two sensitivity checks support the direction of the result, but the estimate is attenuated by nearly one third when opacity degeneracy is modelled explicitly. The excluded or missing-case rate is 5%, and missingness is higher in the subset most relevant to planet-formation inference. The authors also warn that retrieval significance depends on model assumptions about clouds and chemistry. The abstract currently says that the evidence "confirms" a broadly general mechanism. A defensible summary must instead preserve the positive direction of molecular detection significance, mention uncertainty, and avoid turning a bounded dataset into a universal causal claim.
items.prompt: Which interpretation maps the empirical evidence to the most defensible academic claim?
options: The data mainly prove that planet-formation inference deserves more institutional funding, which is a policy conclusion rather than a data-to-text mapping.
Because the confidence interval is positive, the study proves that methane and vertical mixing in a warm Neptune atmosphere will generate the same effect in every future setting.
The sensitivity analysis shows that the primary result was fabricated, not merely dependent on modelling choices.
The missing-data rate can be set aside because the reported sample is large enough to make selection concerns immaterial.

The result is directionally supportive, but missing or excluded cases, opacity degeneracy and the stated limitation mean that the conclusion should remain context-bound rather than causal and universal.
items.rationale: The correct answer is E because it maps the numerical direction of the finding to cautious prose while preserving uncertainty, missingness and sensitivity to opacity degeneracy. The false-synchrony distractor treats sensitivity analysis as proof of fabrication rather than model dependence. The overgeneralisation distractor turns a positive interval into universal causality. The modal-distortion distractor implies sample size automatically removes missing-data bias. The out-of-context funding distractor may sound reasonable outside the passage, but it does not answer the methodological interpretation task.

The reported estimate is 0.11 units, and the 95% confidence interval (0.07, 0.19) does not cross zero. That fact matters, but it is not enough. A specification that includes instrumental systematics makes the result heterogeneous across subgroups; the excluded-case rate reaches 7%; and the evidence comes from unambiguous spectral identification of carbon dioxide rather than from a randomized comparison across every plausible environment. The limitation is explicit: one robust molecule detection is not a complete atmospheric inventory. The strongest data-to-text mapping will therefore translate the result into cautious academic prose, keeping spectral-feature confidence visible while rejecting causal overreach. items.passage: A study in biodiversity monitoring examines environmental DNA as a reporting tool for ecosystem change in the context of biodiversity monitoring programmes. It uses synthesis of sampling design and occupancy-modelling opportunities and reports assessment of how eDNA can support monitoring frameworks. The primary analysis includes 404 observations and estimates an adjusted change of 0.15 units, with a 95% confidence interval from 0.11 to 0.23. dataVisualization.title: Estimate, sensitivity and missingness
dataVisualization.ariaLabel: Mini graph for weather prediction without conventional numerical-weather inputs: adjusted estimate 0.13, confidence interval 0.09 to 0.21, missing or excluded cases 17 percent.
dataVisualization.reviewerCheck: Candidate must integrate direction, uncertainty, sensitivity and missingness rather than reading the estimate alone.

items.passage: A study in atmospheric modelling examines weather prediction without conventional numerical-weather inputs in the context of global weather forecasts. It uses an end-to-end system mapping raw observations directly to forecasts and reports forecast comparisons against conventional numerical weather prediction. The primary analysis includes 732 observations and estimates an adjusted change of 0.13 units, with a 95% confidence interval from 0.09 to 0.21. Two sensitivity checks support the direction of the result, but the estimate is attenuated by nearly one third when observation-network bias is modelled explicitly. The excluded or missing-case rate is 17%, and missingness is higher in the subset most relevant to rapid operational forecasting. The authors also warn that operational independence does not by itself prove robustness under sensor failures. The abstract currently says that the evidence "confirms" a broadly general mechanism. A defensible summary must instead preserve the positive direction of forecast lead-time skill, mention uncertainty, and avoid turning a bounded dataset into a universal causal claim.
items.prompt: Which conclusion best reflects the reported data and its limitations?
options: The sensitivity analysis shows that the primary result was fabricated, not merely dependent on modelling choices.
Because the confidence interval is positive, the study proves that weather prediction without conventional numerical-weather inputs will generate the same effect in every future setting.
The missing-data rate can be set aside because the reported sample is large enough to make selection concerns immaterial.
The data mainly prove that rapid operational forecasting deserves more institutional funding, which is a policy conclusion rather than a data-to-text mapping.

The result is directionally supportive, but missing or excluded cases, observation-network bias and the stated limitation mean that the conclusion should remain context-bound rather than causal and universal.
items.rationale: The correct answer is E because it maps the numerical direction of the finding to cautious prose while preserving uncertainty, missingness and sensitivity to observation-network bias. The false-synchrony distractor treats sensitivity analysis as proof of fabrication rather than model dependence. The overgeneralisation distractor turns a positive interval into universal causality. The modal-distortion distractor implies sample size automatically removes missing-data bias. The out-of-context funding distractor may sound reasonable outside the passage, but it does not answer the methodological interpretation task.

items.passage: The methods paragraph reports a quantitative result for general forecasting across atmospheric and environmental tasks. After adjustment, the measured cross-task transfer skill changes by 0.15 units (95% CI 0.11 to 0.23) in a sample of 773; however, after accounting for benchmark selection, the estimate is directionally stable but less precise. The study design is informative because it uses pretraining on diverse Earth-system data before task-specific adaptation, and the evidence base includes multi-task comparisons across forecasting benchmarks. It is still not unlimited evidence. The missing-data or exclusion rate is 18%, the sampled setting is heterogeneous Earth-system datasets, and the limitation section states that breadth of task coverage can conceal uneven performance in local extremes. The question is how to write the conclusion. The best sentence should neither ignore the observed association nor imply that foundation-model forecasting is ready for unrestricted adoption. The passage rewards attention to modality: it treats the method as consequential, but not as a substitute for validation in heterogeneous Earth-system datasets.

items.passage: In the results section of a recent climate modelling paper, hybrid neural general-circulation modelling is evaluated with a differentiable atmospheric solver combined with learned components. The reported estimate is 0.18 units, and the 95% confidence interval (0.14, 0.26) does not cross zero. That fact matters, but it is not enough. A specification that includes parameterization leakage makes the result heterogeneous across subgroups; the excluded-case rate reaches 19%; and the evidence comes from weather, ensemble and climate simulations compared with established baselines rather than from a randomized comparison across every plausible environment. The limitation is explicit: short-term forecast skill cannot be equated with long-term climate credibility. The strongest data-to-text mapping will therefore translate the result into cautious academic prose, keeping simulation stability visible while rejecting causal overreach. This is why the strongest answer must preserve scope, causal direction and the difference between a research signal and a settled explanation. The relevant academic skill is not recognising the topic, but tracking how the warrant changes when short-term forecast skill cannot be equated with long-term climate credibility.

items.passage: The methods paragraph reports a quantitative result for all-atom prediction of biomolecular complexes. After adjustment, the measured interface confidence changes by 0.15 units (95% CI 0.11 to 0.23) in a sample of 600; however, after accounting for training-set proximity, the estimate is attenuated by nearly one third. The study design is informative because it uses a diffusion-based architecture for joint modelling of proteins, nucleic acids, ligands and ions, and the evidence base includes benchmarking across molecular interaction classes. It is still not unlimited evidence. The missing-data or exclusion rate is 7%, the sampled setting is protein-ligand and protein-nucleic-acid systems, and the limitation section states that confidence scores do not remove the need for physical validation of rare or modified systems. The question is how to write the conclusion. The best sentence should neither ignore the observed association nor imply that drug-discovery triage is ready for unrestricted adoption. A careful reader must therefore distinguish evidential usefulness from unrestricted portability, especially when training-set proximity may alter the observed pattern.
items.prompt: Which interpretation maps the empirical evidence to the most defensible academic claim?
options: The result is directionally supportive, but missing or excluded cases, training-set proximity and the stated limitation mean that the conclusion should remain context-bound rather than causal and universal.
The strongest summary should recommend immediate implementation of drug-discovery triage, even though the question asks about evidential wording.
Excluded cases do not matter once the confidence interval is positive and the main estimate points in the expected direction.
The positive estimate licenses a general causal rule for all-atom prediction of biomolecular complexes, even outside the sampled setting.
The sensitivity analysis cancels the result entirely, even though the passage describes model dependence rather than fraud.
items.rationale: The correct answer is A because it maps the numerical direction of the finding to cautious prose while preserving uncertainty, missingness and sensitivity to training-set proximity. The false-synchrony distractor treats sensitivity analysis as proof of fabrication rather than model dependence. The overgeneralisation distractor turns a positive interval into universal causality. The modal-distortion distractor implies sample size automatically removes missing-data bias. The out-of-context funding distractor may sound reasonable outside the passage, but it does not answer the methodological interpretation task.

items.passage: In the results section of a recent climate informatics paper, probabilistic medium-range weather prediction is evaluated with a generative model trained on decades of atmospheric reanalysis. The reported estimate is 0.18 units, and the 95% confidence interval (0.14, 0.26) does not cross zero. That fact matters, but it is not enough. A specification that includes archive-induced stationarity makes the result directionally stable but less precise; the excluded-case rate reaches 8%; and the evidence comes from comparison with operational ensemble forecasts rather than from a randomized comparison across every plausible environment. The limitation is explicit: rare compound events remain harder to validate than average skill scores imply. The strongest data-to-text mapping will therefore translate the result into cautious academic prose, keeping ensemble reliability visible while rejecting causal overreach. The passage rewards attention to modality: it treats the method as consequential, but not as a substitute for validation in global ten-day forecast cases.

Missingness is only a reporting issue here, so it should not affect the strength of the conclusion.
The result chiefly supports a governance programme for early-warning systems, not a narrower interpretation of the reported estimate.
items.rationale: The correct answer is C because it maps the numerical direction of the finding to cautious prose while preserving uncertainty, missingness and sensitivity to archive-induced stationarity. items.passage: Weather prediction without conventional numerical-weather inputs is reported with a compact numerical summary: 682 observations, an adjusted estimate of 0.20 units, and a 95% interval from 0.16 to 0.28. The method, an end-to-end system mapping raw observations directly to forecasts, makes the study relevant to rapid operational forecasting, and forecast comparisons against conventional numerical weather prediction gives the result empirical weight. The interpretive problem is that the estimate is heterogeneous across subgroups when observation-network bias is included, while the missing-data or exclusion rate reaches 9% and the authors state that operational independence does not by itself prove robustness under sensor failures. A careful abstract sentence would not hide the positive direction of forecast lead-time skill. It would, however, restrict the claim to the observed setting and refuse language that implies universal causality. This is why the strongest answer must preserve scope, causal direction and the difference between a research signal and a settled explanation.

options: The result is directionally supportive, but missing or excluded cases, observation-network bias and the stated limitation mean that the conclusion should remain context-bound rather than causal and universal.

The sensitivity analysis shows that the primary result was fabricated, not merely dependent on modelling choices.
The missing-data rate can be set aside because the reported sample is large enough to make selection concerns immaterial.
The data mainly prove that rapid operational forecasting deserves more institutional funding, which is a policy conclusion rather than a data-to-text mapping.
items.rationale: The correct answer is A because it maps the numerical direction of the finding to cautious prose while preserving uncertainty, missingness and sensitivity to observation-network bias. items.passage: A study in earth-system AI examines general forecasting across atmospheric and environmental tasks in the context of heterogeneous Earth-system datasets. The primary analysis includes 723 observations and estimates an adjusted change of 0.22 units, with a 95% confidence interval from 0.18 to 0.30. items.passage: A study in environmental epidemiology examines hospitalization risk from wildfire-specific fine particulate matter in the context of communities exposed to wildfire PM2.5. It uses multi-country epidemiological modelling of cause-specific respiratory admissions and reports relative-risk comparisons between wildfire and non-wildfire PM2.5. The primary analysis includes 739 observations and estimates an adjusted change of 0.18 units, with a 95% confidence interval from 0.14 to 0.26. Two sensitivity checks support the direction of the result, but the estimate is dependent on the stricter outcome definition when background pollution mixture is modelled explicitly. The excluded or missing-case rate is 7%, and missingness is higher in the subset most relevant to respiratory-health preparedness. The authors also warn that source-specific toxicity does not make every regional risk estimate transferable. The abstract currently says that the evidence "confirms" a broadly general mechanism. A defensible summary must instead preserve the positive direction of cause-specific hospitalization risk, mention uncertainty, and avoid turning a bounded dataset into a universal causal claim.
items.prompt: Which option most accurately converts the results into cautious prose?
options: The result is directionally supportive, but missing or excluded cases, background pollution mixture and the stated limitation mean that the conclusion should remain context-bound rather than causal and universal.
The result chiefly supports a governance programme for respiratory-health preparedness, not a narrower interpretation of the reported estimate.
Missingness is only a reporting issue here, so it should not affect the strength of the conclusion.
The numerical result is sufficient to treat cause-specific hospitalization risk as a settled mechanism rather than a bounded estimate.
Any change under sensitivity testing means the original analysis should be ignored rather than interpreted cautiously.
items.rationale: The correct answer is A because it maps the numerical direction of the finding to cautious prose while preserving uncertainty, missingness and sensitivity to background pollution mixture. The false-synchrony distractor treats sensitivity analysis as proof of fabrication rather than model dependence. The overgeneralisation distractor turns a positive interval into universal causality. The modal-distortion distractor implies sample size automatically removes missing-data bias. The out-of-context funding distractor may sound reasonable outside the passage, but it does not answer the methodological interpretation task.

items.passage: In the results section of a recent exoplanet spectroscopy paper, methane and vertical mixing in a warm Neptune atmosphere is evaluated with JWST transmission spectroscopy with atmospheric retrieval. The reported estimate is 0.20 units, and the 95% confidence interval (0.16, 0.28) does not cross zero. That fact matters, but it is not enough. A specification that includes opacity degeneracy makes the result attenuated by nearly one third; the excluded-case rate reaches 8%; and the evidence comes from molecular detections and abundance estimates for WASP-107b rather than from a randomized comparison across every plausible environment. The limitation is explicit: retrieval significance depends on model assumptions about clouds and chemistry. The strongest data-to-text mapping will therefore translate the result into cautious academic prose, keeping molecular detection significance visible while rejecting causal overreach. A careful reader must therefore distinguish evidential usefulness from unrestricted portability, especially when opacity degeneracy may alter the observed pattern.
items.prompt: Which interpretation maps the empirical evidence to the most defensible academic claim?
options: The sensitivity analysis shows that the primary result was fabricated, not merely dependent on modelling choices.
Because the confidence interval is positive, the study proves that methane and vertical mixing in a warm Neptune atmosphere will generate the same effect in every future setting.
The missing-data rate can be set aside because the reported sample is large enough to make selection concerns immaterial.

The result is directionally supportive, but missing or excluded cases, opacity degeneracy and the stated limitation mean that the conclusion should remain context-bound rather than causal and universal.
The data mainly prove that planet-formation inference deserves more institutional funding, which is a policy conclusion rather than a data-to-text mapping.
items.rationale: The correct answer is D because it maps the numerical direction of the finding to cautious prose while preserving uncertainty, missingness and sensitivity to opacity degeneracy. The false-synchrony distractor treats sensitivity analysis as proof of fabrication rather than model dependence. The overgeneralisation distractor turns a positive interval into universal causality. items.passage: A study in multimodal pathology examines whole-slide modelling linked to reports and rare-cancer retrieval in the context of diverse pathology slides and reports. It uses joint learning from image and text representations and reports linear-probing, few-shot, zero-shot and cross-modal retrieval evaluations. The primary analysis includes 878 observations and estimates an adjusted change of 0.20 units, with a 95% confidence interval from 0.16 to 0.28. Two sensitivity checks support the direction of the result, but the estimate is heterogeneous across subgroups when report-style regularity is modelled explicitly. The excluded or missing-case rate is 7%, and missingness is higher in the subset most relevant to pathology report support. The authors also warn that cross-modal success does not guarantee calibrated clinical reasoning. The abstract currently says that the evidence "confirms" a broadly general mechanism. A defensible summary must instead preserve the positive direction of cross-modal retrieval performance, mention uncertainty, and avoid turning a bounded dataset into a universal causal claim.
items.prompt: Which statement preserves uncertainty, direction, and scope?
options: The result is directionally supportive, but missing or excluded cases, report-style regularity and the stated limitation mean that the conclusion should remain context-bound rather than causal and universal.
The data mainly prove that pathology report support deserves more institutional funding, which is a policy conclusion rather than a data-to-text mapping.
Because the confidence interval is positive, the study proves that whole-slide modelling linked to reports and rare-cancer retrieval will generate the same effect in every future setting.
The missing-data rate can be set aside because the reported sample is large enough to make selection concerns immaterial.
The sensitivity analysis shows that the primary result was fabricated, not merely dependent on modelling choices.
items.rationale: The correct answer is A because it maps the numerical direction of the finding to cautious prose while preserving uncertainty, missingness and sensitivity to report-style regularity. The false-synchrony distractor treats sensitivity analysis as proof of fabrication rather than model dependence. The overgeneralisation distractor turns a positive interval into universal causality. The modal-distortion distractor implies sample size automatically removes missing-data bias. The out-of-context funding distractor may sound reasonable outside the passage, but it does not answer the methodological interpretation task.

items.passage: The methods paragraph reports a quantitative result for replicability of online behavioural experiments. After adjustment, the measured replication effect ratio changes by 0.22 units (95% CI 0.18 to 0.30) in a sample of 279; however, after accounting for original-study selection, the estimate is dependent on the stricter outcome definition. The study design is informative because it uses preregistered replications selected with prediction-market information, and the evidence base includes effect-size shrinkage and alternative replication indicators. It is still not unlimited evidence. The missing-data or exclusion rate is 8%, the sampled setting is online behavioural studies, and the limitation section states that market prediction improves triage without replacing independent replication. The question is how to write the conclusion. The best sentence should neither ignore the observed association nor imply that replication prioritisation is ready for unrestricted adoption. The relevant academic skill is not recognising the topic, but tracking how the warrant changes when market prediction improves triage without replacing independent replication.

items.passage: In the results section of a recent research evaluation paper, forecasting which claims will replicate is evaluated with elicited expert and crowd predictions compared with replication outcomes. The reported estimate is 0.24 units, and the 95% confidence interval (0.20, 0.32) does not cross zero. That fact matters, but it is not enough. A specification that includes predictor information asymmetry makes the result attenuated by nearly one third; the excluded-case rate reaches 9%; and the evidence comes from prediction accuracy assessed against later replication evidence rather than from a randomized comparison across every plausible environment. The limitation is explicit: forecasting reliability still depends on claim selection and elicitation design. The strongest data-to-text mapping will therefore translate the result into cautious academic prose, keeping replicability forecast accuracy visible while rejecting causal overreach. A careful reader must therefore distinguish evidential usefulness from unrestricted portability, especially when predictor information asymmetry may alter the observed pattern.

Missingness is only a reporting issue here, so it should not affect the strength of the conclusion.
The numerical result is sufficient to treat replicability forecast accuracy as a settled mechanism rather than a bounded estimate.
Any change under sensitivity testing means the original analysis should be ignored rather than interpreted cautiously.
items.rationale: The correct answer is B because it maps the numerical direction of the finding to cautious prose while preserving uncertainty, missingness and sensitivity to predictor information asymmetry. items.passage: In the results section of a recent climate attribution paper, wildfire particulate-matter deaths attributable to climate change is evaluated with counterfactual attribution of fire emissions and health burden. The reported estimate is 0.22 units, and the 95% confidence interval (0.18, 0.30) does not cross zero. That fact matters, but it is not enough. A specification that includes baseline air-quality trend makes the result attenuated by nearly one third; the excluded-case rate reaches 17%; and the evidence comes from estimated deaths and economic burden over multiple fire seasons rather than from a randomized comparison across every plausible environment. The limitation is explicit: attribution depends on exposure-response assumptions and fire-emissions modelling. The strongest data-to-text mapping will therefore translate the result into cautious academic prose, keeping attributable mortality visible while rejecting causal overreach. items.passage: A study in conservation genomics examines environmental-DNA surveys for restoration planning in the context of aquatic species in restoration watersheds. items.passage: A study in robotics examines dexterous manipulation learned with human-in-the-loop reinforcement learning in the context of robotic manipulation tasks. It uses vision-based reinforcement learning with human interventions and reports performance across dexterous manipulation tasks. The primary analysis includes 377 observations and estimates an adjusted change of 0.22 units, with a 95% confidence interval from 0.18 to 0.30. Two sensitivity checks support the direction of the result, but the estimate is directionally stable but less precise when operator strategy bias is modelled explicitly. The excluded or missing-case rate is 7%, and missingness is higher in the subset most relevant to general-purpose dexterity. The authors also warn that intervention efficiency matters as much as final task success. The abstract currently says that the evidence "confirms" a broadly general mechanism. A defensible summary must instead preserve the positive direction of intervention-free completion, mention uncertainty, and avoid turning a bounded dataset into a universal causal claim.
items.prompt: Which conclusion best reflects the reported data and its limitations?
options: The result is directionally supportive, but missing or excluded cases, operator strategy bias and the stated limitation mean that the conclusion should remain context-bound rather than causal and universal.
The positive estimate licenses a general causal rule for dexterous manipulation learned with human-in-the-loop reinforcement learning, even outside the sampled setting.
The strongest summary should recommend immediate implementation of general-purpose dexterity, even though the question asks about evidential wording.
The sensitivity analysis cancels the result entirely, even though the passage describes model dependence rather than fraud.
Excluded cases do not matter once the confidence interval is positive and the main estimate points in the expected direction.
items.rationale: The correct answer is A because it maps the numerical direction of the finding to cautious prose while preserving uncertainty, missingness and sensitivity to operator strategy bias. The false-synchrony distractor treats sensitivity analysis as proof of fabrication rather than model dependence. The overgeneralisation distractor turns a positive interval into universal causality. The modal-distortion distractor implies sample size automatically removes missing-data bias. The out-of-context funding distractor may sound reasonable outside the passage, but it does not answer the methodological interpretation task.

items.passage: The methods paragraph reports a quantitative result for visuotactile object manipulation transferred from simulation. After adjustment, the measured placement success rate changes by 0.24 units (95% CI 0.20 to 0.32) in a sample of 418; however, after accounting for contact-sensor calibration, the estimate is heterogeneous across subgroups. The study design is informative because it uses simulation-trained policies integrating visual and tactile information, and the evidence base includes pick, localization, regrasp and placement performance. It is still not unlimited evidence. The missing-data or exclusion rate is 8%, the sampled setting is object manipulation trials, and the limitation section states that simulation precision can fail when contact conditions shift. The question is how to write the conclusion. The best sentence should neither ignore the observed association nor imply that precise industrial handling is ready for unrestricted adoption. This is why the strongest answer must preserve scope, causal direction and the difference between a research signal and a settled explanation.
items.prompt: Which statement preserves uncertainty, direction, and scope?
options: The result chiefly supports a governance programme for precise industrial handling, not a narrower interpretation of the reported estimate.
Missingness is only a reporting issue here, so it should not affect the strength of the conclusion.
Any change under sensitivity testing means the original analysis should be ignored rather than interpreted cautiously.
The numerical result is sufficient to treat placement success rate as a settled mechanism rather than a bounded estimate.

The result is directionally supportive, but missing or excluded cases, contact-sensor calibration and the stated limitation mean that the conclusion should remain context-bound rather than causal and universal.
items.rationale: The correct answer is E because it maps the numerical direction of the finding to cautious prose while preserving uncertainty, missingness and sensitivity to contact-sensor calibration. The false-synchrony distractor treats sensitivity analysis as proof of fabrication rather than model dependence. The overgeneralisation distractor turns a positive interval into universal causality. The modal-distortion distractor implies sample size automatically removes missing-data bias. The out-of-context funding distractor may sound reasonable outside the passage, but it does not answer the methodological interpretation task.

items.passage: In the results section of a recent embodied AI paper, reinforcement-learned humanoid locomotion in the real world is evaluated with a learning-based controller using proprioceptive history. The reported estimate is 0.07 units, and the 95% confidence interval (0.03, 0.15) does not cross zero. That fact matters, but it is not enough. A specification that includes terrain-selection bias makes the result dependent on the stricter outcome definition; the excluded-case rate reaches 9%; and the evidence comes from locomotion performance outside purely simulated settings rather than from a randomized comparison across every plausible environment. The limitation is explicit: real-world stability must be distinguished from scripted demonstration success. The strongest data-to-text mapping will therefore translate the result into cautious academic prose, keeping recovery-after-disturbance rate visible while rejecting causal overreach. The relevant academic skill is not recognising the topic, but tracking how the warrant changes when real-world stability must be distinguished from scripted demonstration success.
items.prompt: Which option most accurately converts the results into cautious prose?
options: The data mainly prove that adaptive robot mobility deserves more institutional funding, which is a policy conclusion rather than a data-to-text mapping.
The missing-data rate can be set aside because the reported sample is large enough to make selection concerns immaterial.

The result is directionally supportive, but missing or excluded cases, terrain-selection bias and the stated limitation mean that the conclusion should remain context-bound rather than causal and universal.
The sensitivity analysis shows that the primary result was fabricated, not merely dependent on modelling choices.
Because the confidence interval is positive, the study proves that reinforcement-learned humanoid locomotion in the real world will generate the same effect in every future setting.
items.rationale: The correct answer is C because it maps the numerical direction of the finding to cautious prose while preserving uncertainty, missingness and sensitivity to terrain-selection bias. The false-synchrony distractor treats sensitivity analysis as proof of fabrication rather than model dependence. The overgeneralisation distractor turns a positive interval into universal causality. The modal-distortion distractor implies sample size automatically removes missing-data bias. The out-of-context funding distractor may sound reasonable outside the passage, but it does not answer the methodological interpretation task.
